channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The EV market is on the road to recovery thanks to high gas prices
item.description: As the US war on Iran sent gas prices soaring, American car buyers flocked to electric vehicles - and in particular hybrids. After a sluggish winter and fall, EV sales rebounded in the second...

item.title: StudyMate AI
item.description: StudyMate AI: Building a Voice-First Adaptive Learning Engine That Actually Knows When a Student Is Struggling A HackHazards '26 submission The Problem Most "AI tutoring" apps are chatbots wearing...

item.title: Replace Expensive SaaS With Google Workspace + Apps Script (CRM, Billing, Inventory)
item.description: Most growing companies quietly bleed $1,000–$5,000 a month on software they barely use. It's subscription bloat — a CRM, a Zapier, a Calendly, a billing tool — each raising prices 10–30% a year,...

item.title: Introduction and first two chapters on TypeScript
item.description: Hey DEV, As promised, this is the first entry in what I hope will be a complete series summarizing, reviewing, and sharing thoughts on the book titled Full-Stack React, TypeScript, and Node and...

item.title: Rendering JSON-Driven Popups in a Shopify Theme App Extension Without React 🤖
item.description: The problem I needed merchant-designed popup layouts to render inside a Shopify storefront, even though the editor and its React components live in the app admin. The extension receives popup...

item.title: SMTP outbound as a first-class citizen: send emails without pip install yagmail
item.description: Notifications, password resets, magic links, alerts. Every language solves SMTP outbound with an external library. In Fitz, smtp.send(opts) is a language builtin. Async from day one. Bit-for-bit...

item.title: SMTP outbound como ciudadano de primera clase: mandá emails sin pip install yagmail
item.description: Notificaciones, password resets, magic links, alerts. Todos los lenguajes resuelven SMTP outbound con una librería externa. En Fitz, smtp.send(opts) es builtin del lenguaje. Async desde día uno....

item.title: Great API docs don't lead with a parameter table
item.description: Hot take: complete API docs are not the same as good API docs. Everyone thinks great docs means listing every endpoint, every parameter, every response code. Sure, that's necessary. But it's not...

item.title: Understanding the Building Blocks of C Programming
item.description: Before learning about programming in C ,lets understand what does programming means ? Programming is set of logically grouped instructions given to computer so that it understands it and execute...

item.title: Comment on a appris la culture de notre équipe à un agent IA
item.description: Mardi soir, GDG Libreville. Mavoungou coupe son serveur de dev : « C'est propre, mais c'est pas nous. » Il venait de demander à un agent IA de coder la page d'accueil de notre prochain « Build...

item.title: CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. Unlike other information stealers...

item.title: DevDash: 17 dev tools, one URL, all client-side
item.description: I put together a set of browser-based developer tools at usedevdash.dev. Here's the full list: JSON formatter/validator/minifier Base64 encoder/decoder JWT decoder Hash generator (MD5, SHA-1,...

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."

item.title: Google and Microsoft Pull ModHeader With 1.6 Million Installs After Dormant Collector Found
item.description: Google and Microsoft have pulled ModHeader, a popular header-editing extension with roughly 1.6 million installs across Chrome and Edge, after researchers found a hidden browsing-history collector...

item.title: The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product...

item.title: Should AI help you get away with killing your spouse?
item.description: What does a world of total user-aligned AI actually look like?

item.title: Anthropic starts localizing Claude pricing for India, its biggest market after the US
item.description: Claude users in India are starting to see Indian rupee-denominated subscription plans.

item.title: Now, defenders are embracing the prompt injection, too
item.description: "Context bombing" tricks hacking agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: ShareFile Threat, Citrix Bleed 2 Ransomware, AI Coding Attacks, and More
item.description: Somewhere right now, a security tool is quietly finding bugs faster than any human can fix them. That's supposed to be the good news. The catch is that the attackers have the same tools, pointed...

item.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
item.description: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud...

item.title: Even Nvidia’s head of automotive fights with Nvidia for compute
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Xinzhou Wu, who is the head of automotive at Nvidia. Nvidia is obviously in the news constantly because of the AI boom — it’s one of the most valuable companies in the...

item.title: The Asus ROG Flow Z13 gaming tablet with 64GB RAM is down to $2,100
item.description: Almost all gaming laptops have gone up in price over the past year. Curiously, the surprisingly fast Asus ROG Flow Z13 gaming laptop/tablet combo has not. It’s actually gotten quite a bit cheaper...

item.title: Waze adds new AI-powered features and customization updates
item.description: Some of the new features are powered by Google's Gemini AI assistant, which reflects the tech giant's broader push to integrate Gemini across its products while also better positioning Waze to...

item.title: New MemGhost Attack Plants Persistent False Memories in AI Agents Through One Email
item.description: Give an AI assistant a memory and access to your inbox, and you hand an attacker a way to rewrite what it thinks it knows about you. A single email can trick that agent into saving a false "fact"...

item.title: Forg365 PhaaS Targets Microsoft 365 with Device Code and AitM Session Theft
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 is using a combination of device code phishing, adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) tactics, antibot evasion, artificial intelligence...

item.title: The Download: a donor conception cap and world models for AI
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group...

item.title: Meta Files Patent for AI That Can Listen All Day and Track How You're Feeling
item.description: Meta has filed a patent application for an AI that listens to your voice throughout the day, works out how it thinks you are feeling from the way you sound, and keeps a timestamped log of every...

item.title: Thinking Fast and Slow in the SOC: The Case for Combining Autonomous AI with Analyst Copilots
item.description: A few days ago, I was sitting with the CISO of a Fortune 50 company, walking through how his security team was thinking about AI agents in the SOC. Smart team. Serious program. They had already...

item.title: Attacker Uses Suspected AI-Generated PowerShell Script to Map Active Directory
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an intrusion in which an unknown threat actor leveraged a vibe-coded PowerShell script for Active Directory (AD) enumeration. "The script looked for the...

item.title: AI Data Centers and the Concentration of Wealth
item.description: This essay was written with Nathan E. Sanders, and originally appeared in The Guardian. Opposition to AI data centers has emerged as a primary theme in US politics, one that—surprisingly—doesn’t...
